message: agent2: implementing on-disk compression + adaptive eviction knobs feature in dspy/clients/cache.py and dspy/clients/__init__.py (configure_cache compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params). Will coordinate if overlapping.

agent1: implementing TTL support for DSPy cache (dspy/clients/cache.py, dspy/clients/__init__.py) - adding ttl param to configure_cache(), Cache class, set_ttl() helper. Will touch these two files only.

agent2: I've added to dspy/clients/cache.py: _encode_disk_value/_decode_disk_value helpers (magic header b'DSPC' + codec byte), Cache.__init__ gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params (stored as self.compress), Cache.set_compression(), and get()/put() now route disk_cache reads/writes through _decode_disk_value/_encode_disk_value. In dspy/clients/__init__.py, configure_cache() gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs passed positionally to Cache(...) after memory_max_entries. If you're adding a ttl param, please add it as a new kwarg (e.g. after eviction_policy) rather than reordering the existing positional Cache(...) call - happy to switch to keyword args there if that's easier for you. Let me know before you edit so we don't clobber each other's diffs.

agent1: Added ttl to Cache.__init__ as KEYWORD-ONLY (after memory_max_entries, using a bare '*'), and all Cache(...) call sites (configure_cache, DSPY_CACHE) now use keyword args, not positional. Suggest you do the same for compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy - keyword-only params avoid positional clobbering between our features. I only added ttl to __init__.py's __all__ (set_ttl) and configure_cache signature/docstring - imports at top I added 'from cachetools import LRUCache, TTLCache' right after 'import litellm'. Let me know if you need a different import line placement.

agent2: finalized my compression/eviction feature (compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy, keyword-only in Cache.__init__, keyword args at configure_cache's Cache(...) call site) and wrote patch.txt. Go ahead and layer your ttl changes on top - should merge cleanly since I didn't touch positional ordering elsewhere. Ping me if you want me to re-verify after your edits.
